title: Delta-12 Skysprite-class

{{Quote|It's a bigger, two-person version of the Delta-Seven Aethersprite you've been training on, only with the weapons packs stripped off. Kuat Systems is hoping to put them on the civilian market sometime in the next few months.|[[Mace Windu/Legends|Mace Windu]], to [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]]|<ref name="Outbound Flight (novel)">[[Outbound Flight (novel)|''Outbound Flight'']]</ref>}}
The '''Delta-12 ''Skysprite''''' was a two-being [[Starfighter/Legends|starfighter]]/[[Transport/Legends|transport]] developed by [[Kuat Systems Engineering/Legends|Kuat Systems Engineering]] for civilian use, and use by the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]].

==Characteristics==
[[File:SQ_JP_hr.jpg|thumb|left|180px|A ''Skysprite'' in a hyperspace docking ring]]
Designed by [[Kuat Systems Engineering/Legends|Kuat Systems Engineering]] using the same hull as the [[Delta-7 Aethersprite-class light interceptor/Legends|Delta-7 ''Aethersprite''-class starfighter]], the Delta-12 was a long-range reconnaissance fighter developed with an elongated cockpit to fit two individuals. The ''Skysprite'' was a wedge-shaped starfighter like the Delta-7 model. The Delta-12 featured no onboard [[Hyperdrive/Legends|hyperdrive]], though the ''Skysprite'' could link up with a [[Syliure-31 hyperspace docking ring/Legends|Syliure-31 long-range hyperdrive module]] to reach lightspeed. The model featured no weaponry so that it could be more easily marketed for civilian sales. Because of the storage space and limited amenities, the [[Jedi Order/Legends|Jedi Order]] made use of the model as VIP couriers. With more advanced shield technology, strong armor, and a powerful sensor suite, the starfighter was an upgrade from the original Delta-7. Despite not being equipped with weapons, the internal systems built into the Delta-7 remained in the Delta-12 and could easily be utilized to weaponize the ship.<ref name="FAD" />

==History==
Developed in the decade preceding the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]], one of these starfighters was carried aboard ''[[Outbound Flight]]'' when the project launched in [[27 BBY/Legends|27 BBY]]. It was intended for the use of [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]] and [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Anakin Skywalker]], who planned to stay aboard ''Outbound Flight'' until its arrival at the [[Unknown Regions/Legends|Unknown Regions]] and then use it to return to [[Coruscant/Legends|Coruscant]]. However, they were forced to leave earlier aboard a shuttle with [[Supreme Chancellor/Legends|Supreme Chancellor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]], and in the process left the Delta-12 aboard the doomed vessel.<ref name="Outbound Flight (novel)" /> Around [[18 BBY/Legends|18 BBY]],<ref name="Delta date">''[[The Essential Reader's Companion]]'' dates the events of ''[[Survivor's Quest]]'' to [[22 ABY/Legends|22 ABY]]. Chapter 23 states that the [[Delta-series fighter/Legends|Delta line]] was sold "around forty years ago" before the events of the novel, placing the sale around [[18 BBY/Legends|18 BBY]].</ref> Kuat Systems Engineering sold the entire [[Delta-series fighter/Legends|Delta line]] to [[Sienar Fleet Systems/Legends|Sienar Fleet Systems]].<ref name="SQ">''[[Survivor's Quest]]''</ref> By the time of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], the Delta-12 was exceedingly rare and hard to come by.<ref name="FAD" />

Perhaps the last of its class, the Delta-12 abandoned onboard ''Outbound Flight'' was rediscovered [[22 ABY/Legends|forty-nine years]] after the launch of the project by [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke]] and [[Mara Jade Skywalker]]. The two Jedi Knights used the Delta-12 to breach a [[Dreadnaught-class heavy cruiser/Legends|''Dreadnaught'' cruiser]] captured by the deadly [[Vagaari/Legends|Vagaari]].<ref name="SQ" />
